desc: Budapest straddles the Danube between hilly Buda and grand Pest boulevards, combining imperial architecture, thermal-bath culture, and one of Central Europe's strongest nightlife scenes. Visitor demand is typically highest from late spring into early autumn and during festive December periods, with shoulder months often offering the best value-weather balance.
hls: Historic thermal baths across the city
Castle Hill and Fisherman’s Bastion views
Ruin bar nightlife in the Jewish Quarter
Strong value for a major European capital

todo.name: Parliament riverfront and promenade walk
todo.desc: See Budapest’s signature neo-Gothic facade from both street level and opposite-bank viewpoints.

todo.name: Soak at Széchenyi or Gellért Baths
todo.desc: Experience classic thermal bathing culture in ornate historic pools and steam rooms.

todo.name: Castle Hill and Matthias Church
todo.desc: Explore medieval and baroque layers, then pause at Fisherman’s Bastion terraces.

todo.name: Danube evening cruise
todo.desc: Take a night sail for illuminated bridges, Buda Castle, and Parliament reflections.

todo.name: Great Market Hall and local specialties
todo.desc: Try lángos, paprika goods, and Hungarian pastries while browsing the city’s best-known indoor market.

todo.name: Ruin bars in District VII
todo.desc: Hop between eclectic bars built in former courtyards and old apartment blocks.

wifi.note: Excellent citywide connectivity across apartments, cafés, coworking spaces, and central transit corridors.

prac.fltNote: Direct flights are available from Gatwick; London City typically requires a connection. Return fares are often ~£40-£110 off-peak, ~£110-£190 in shoulder demand, and ~£190+ around summer and Christmas-market peaks.
prac.visa: Hungary is in Schengen; UK and US passport holders can generally stay visa-free up to 90 days in any 180-day period.
alerts: Popular thermal baths and river cruises can sell out on peak weekends and holiday periods.
High summer afternoons can be hot; plan long hill climbs and exposed viewpoints for morning/evening.
Major festivals and December market weeks can significantly raise central accommodation prices.

sweet: May-June and September-early October are usually best for walkability, bath culture, and riverfront evenings without peak midsummer heat.
